assertions.text: V1 was traveling east on a two-lane undivided roadway.
V1 departed the roadway to the right (i.e., off the eastbound lane onto the right shoulder or beyond).
The front of V1 contacted a bridge abutment (frontal impact with stationary bridge abutment).
V1 came to final rest at the point of impact (final rest location coincides with the bridge abutment contact point).
There was a single impact event described (front of V1 contacting the abutment).
V1 departed the roadway to the right before the frontal contact with the bridge abutment.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry provides vehicle speed values [77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and 87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and vehicle metadata stating travel on a road with speed limit [56.0 km/h](T1), but there is no GPS heading or lane data to determine that V1 was traveling east. Therefore direction 'east' is [unverifiable] from sensors.

There is no lateral position, GPS track, or documented lane/shoulder sensor in the data to indicate a departure to the right. The crash pulse shows lateral cumulative delta-V up to [11.0 km/h at t=0.04 s](T3) and final lateral delta-V [−1.0 km/h](T2), but this alone does not prove a roadway departure to the right, so the claim is [unverifiable].

The measured cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaches a negative maximum of [−87.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T2), indicating a large deceleration consistent with a frontal impact; lateral delta-V peak is smaller ([−11.0 km/h at t=0.04 s](T3)). The brake channel shows no application before impact ([fraction_on = 0.0, first=0, last=0](T2)), consistent with uncontrolled forward motion into a fixed object. Together these telemetry signatures support a frontal impact with a stationary object like a bridge abutment.

EDR summary lists a single recorded event ([num_events_recorded = 1](T1)) and the delta-V final longitudinal value is [−87.0 km/h](T2), but there is no absolute position or post-impact movement trace to confirm the final rest location coincides with the abutment contact point; therefore this is [unverifiable].

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded = 1](T1) and detect_events found a single dominant crash-pulse in longitudinal delta-V with magnitude [87.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T3), consistent with one impact event.

Pre-impact speed samples show speed rising from [77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and steering shows a peak raw value of [−78.0 deg at t=0.0 s](T2), but there is no lateral position or timestamped GPS path to confirm the sequence 'depart roadway to right' occurred before the frontal contact; therefore the sequence is [unverifiable].

result.rationale: Telemetry clearly shows a single, large frontal crash pulse with [−87.0 km/h peak longitudinal delta-V](T2) and no pre-impact braking ([brake fraction_on=0.0](T2)), supporting a frontal impact. However, heading, lane departure, and final rest-location relative to the abutment cannot be determined from the available channels, leaving several narrative assertions unverifiable.

citations.claim: 77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and 87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s
77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s to 87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
